sample_information.facility_residential_.answer: Oceanside Villas Condominium Complex, Building B – 145 Chalan San Antonio, Tamuning, GU 96913

sample_information.notes_instructions.answer:
This sample was collected from the primary cold-water kitchen tap in Unit B-304 at Oceanside Villas Condominium Complex. The unit is currently occupied by a family with two young children (ages 3 and 6), and the parents have reported a persistent slight metallic taste and occasional discoloration (faint yellowish tint) in the tap water over the past two months, especially in the early morning after the line has not been used overnight.

Sampling procedure details: The kitchen faucet aerator was removed prior to sampling to minimize particulate interference. The cold water was allowed to run for approximately 5 minutes to flush the line until the temperature stabilized and any visible discoloration cleared. A pre-labeled, laboratory-supplied, 1-liter sterile plastic sampling bottle with tamper-evident seal was used. The bottle was rinsed once with the sample water (except for the preserved bottle intended for metals, which was not rinsed per standard protocol) and then filled to the indicated fill line, leaving minimal headspace. The cap was secured tightly and the tamper seal was applied immediately. A duplicate 250 mL sample was also collected for potential follow-up metals analysis, although only the primary sample is being submitted at this time.

The client specifically requested analysis for basic potability parameters, including total coliform and E. coli, pH, turbidity, total dissolved solids, and free chlorine residual, to provide documentation to concerned residents and to compare with Guam EPA and US EPA drinking water standards. Although Guam Waterworks Authority has indicated that the distribution system is in compliance, the property management company would like independent confirmation for this particular building due to its older internal plumbing (copper with some sections of galvanized pipe reportedly replaced in the late 1990s).

Sample transport: Immediately after collection, the bottle was placed in a clean insulated cooler with partially melted ice packs to maintain a temperature near 4°C. The cooler remained in the custody of the sampler (Maria L. Cruz) at all times until delivery to the UOG WERI Water Quality Laboratory reception area. Approximate holding time from collection to delivery was just under 2 hours. No preservatives other than those pre-added by the laboratory in the bottle (if any) were introduced by the sampler.

Special instructions: If any parameters exceed Guam or US EPA primary drinking water standards, please note this clearly in the report and, if possible, provide a brief interpretive comment suitable for sharing with non-technical condominium residents. If microbiological contamination is detected, the property manager requests to be notified by email as soon as preliminary results are available, even prior to the final written report, so they can coordinate with Guam Waterworks Authority and inform residents promptly.

Please retain the sample for the maximum allowable holding time for microbiological and basic chemical analyses in case follow-up tests (e.g., specific metals such as lead, copper, and zinc) are requested after initial results are reviewed.

analysis_requested.specific_description_.answer: Kitchen cold-water tap, Unit B-304, Oceanside Villas Condominium Complex, Building B – 145 Chalan San Antonio, Tamuning, GU 96913 (3rd floor corner unit, tap closest to building’s main cold-water riser)

analysis_requested.volume.answer: 1 L (primary sample bottle) + 250 mL (retained duplicate, not submitted)
